scope: Embedded Neovim BUFFER implementation from docs/embedded-neovim-buffer.md

approvedExceptions.reason: This is the deliberate user-facing inline fallback status for a Vim command that the inline provider does not implement.
This method normalizes thrown provider input errors and uses TypeScript's unknown type intentionally.

requiredBehaviors: defines a provider API for open, close, save, force save, dirty state, status, input, click, resize, focus, cleanup, snapshots, and capability reporting
selects embedded Neovim as the default provider when discovery reports a usable binary
selects the inline provider only when embedded Neovim is unusable or configuration explicitly requests inline mode
keeps the external editor path as an explicit provider action instead of the default BUFFER mode
reports capability flags truthfully, including exact Vim semantics only for the embedded Neovim provider
edgeCases: provider selection returns inline fallback with a concrete reason when discovery returns a missing binary result
provider selection returns inline fallback with a concrete reason when discovery returns an unsupported version result
provider selection honors an explicit inline configuration even when a usable binary exists
capabilities for the inline provider never mark exact Vim semantics as available
closing a selected provider calls its cleanup path exactly once when the surface changes quickly

requiredBehaviors: discovers the configured Neovim executable before searching PATH
executes the candidate with a bounded version probe and parses the reported version
accepts only binaries that support embedded mode
returns structured fallback reasons for missing executables, failed probes, timeouts, and unsupported versions
prefers user init loading for default embedded spawn arguments and falls back to embedded clean mode when default startup fails
edgeCases: an empty configured executable is treated as absent and PATH discovery is still attempted
a probe that exits nonzero records stderr in the fallback reason without throwing
a probe that hangs past the timeout kills the probe process and returns a timeout result
a version line with extra suffix text still parses the numeric major minor patch values
clean embedded mode is selected when configuration disables user init loading
default user init loading falls back to clean embedded mode when the initial embedded startup probe fails

requiredBehaviors: encodes Neovim msgpack-RPC requests and notifications, and decodes responses and errors using request ids
decodes streaming msgpack data split across arbitrary stdout chunks
resolves requests only when the matching response id arrives
dispatches Neovim notifications to registered handlers without blocking request resolution
rejects pending requests when the process exits or the transport closes
edgeCases: two responses arriving out of order resolve the matching pending requests
a response with an error object rejects the matching request with method and id context
a malformed RPC frame is surfaced as a transport error and closes pending requests
a notification for an unregistered method is recorded without crashing the transport
calling request after close rejects immediately and writes no bytes
transport close is the cancellation boundary for all pending requests

requiredBehaviors: attaches Neovim as an external UI with line grid support sized to the BUFFER pane
reduces grid line, clear, scroll, resize, highlight, cursor, mode, command line, popup menu, and message events into a render snapshot
bounds rendered rows and columns to the current pane size
preserves cell text, width, highlight id, and cursor metadata
updates mode and command line state from Neovim UI events without interpreting Vim commands in AgenC
edgeCases: a grid resize smaller than existing content trims rows and cursor coordinates to valid bounds
a line grid event containing double width cells occupies the correct number of columns
a scroll event with negative rows moves content in the correct direction inside the bounded grid
a cursor event targeting an inactive grid does not corrupt the active grid cursor
a command line hide event clears command line state while preserving normal mode text

requiredBehaviors: translates printable input, named keys, escape, control keys, alt keys, shift modified keys, paste, mouse, focus, and resize into Neovim input or UI calls
routes editor-owned keys to the active provider before workbench or composer keybindings handle them
keeps global escape, BUFFER escape hatches, and surface switching behavior explicit and conflict free
routes mouse wheel and clicks only to the pane under the pointer
sends resize events to Neovim when the BUFFER pane dimensions change
delegates colon command input such as :wq to embedded Neovim instead of parsing it through the inline Vim command layer
edgeCases: escape in insert mode is forwarded to Neovim instead of closing the workbench surface
ctrl-s triggers provider save without inserting control text into the buffer
bracketed paste forwards the pasted text as one paste operation with paste mode boundaries
mouse wheel over BUFFER does not scroll the workspace explorer
mouse wheel events without parseable pointer coordinates do not get captured by BUFFER
click outside the BUFFER pane does not call provider click and can be handled by the pane under the pointer
Workbench surface-switching chords while BUFFER is focused do not move workbench focus away from the editor
BUFFER escape-hatch chords while embedded Neovim owns keys can return focus to composer, explorer, or agents
embedded command input for :wq forwards colon, letters, and enter to Neovim exactly as editor input
resize to one row and one column sends a valid Neovim resize request

requiredBehaviors: opens a file in supervised embedded Neovim, configures embedded editing defaults, and attaches UI before accepting input
implements save, force save, quit, force quit, dirty close prevention, surface switch cleanup, app exit cleanup, and crash cleanup
tracks dirty state from Neovim buffer options and write events
prevents orphaned Neovim child processes after normal close, parent exit, and test failure
surfaces lifecycle errors in provider status without crashing the whole TUI
edgeCases: dirty quit returns a refusal status while keeping the provider alive
force quit discards edits and resolves cleanup after Neovim exits
process exit before UI attach rejects open and clears pending requests
embedded startup enables true-color, syntax, and filetype behavior before dirty tracking begins
double close waits on one cleanup path and does not send duplicate quit commands
startup failure after spawn but before UI attach completes kills the spawned child process
dirty surface switch is refused before BUFFER unmount cleanup can discard embedded Neovim edits
BufferSurface unmount on surface switch cleans up the active provider instead of leaving an embedded Neovim process alive
provider replacement waits for active Neovim cleanup before opening the replacement provider
superseded Neovim open after session start cleans up the stale spawned session
cleanup during in-flight Neovim startup cancels the open and cleans up the late spawned session
cleanup during in-flight Neovim startup ignores a late start failure without publishing stale errors
parent cleanup kills the child process group when Neovim ignores graceful quit

requiredBehaviors: resolves file paths safely relative to the AgenC cwd before any provider opens a file
rejects binary files and files over the configured size limit before embedded Neovim launches when possible
preserves encoding and line ending metadata across read and write paths
detects disk conflicts and in-flight agent edit conflicts deterministically before saving
keeps active file and cursor state synchronized after provider cursor movement and file writes
edgeCases: a path escaping the workspace through parent segments is rejected before provider open
a file containing a NUL byte is rejected with a user-facing binary file message
a file over the maximum size returns a size-specific error before Neovim spawn
a workspace symlink resolving outside the workspace is rejected before file reads
a disk mtime change after open blocks non-force save with conflict status
a CRLF file saved without text changes keeps CRLF line endings

requiredBehaviors: preserves existing AgenC-side LSP open, change, save, and close notifications for inline fallback buffers
keeps LSP notification failures from breaking inline editing or changing visible buffer status
marks inline fallback as the only provider with AgenC-side LSP passthrough
marks embedded Neovim as not claiming AgenC-side LSP passthrough until a real Neovim LSP bridge exists
edgeCases: opening an inline fallback buffer notifies the LSP bridge with absolute path and content
editing inline fallback text notifies the LSP bridge with the updated document text
saving and closing inline fallback buffers notify the LSP bridge in order
a thrown LSP notification does not revert inline buffer edits or set an error state
embedded Neovim capabilities explicitly report LSP passthrough as unavailable

requiredBehaviors: renders provider snapshots inside the bounded BUFFER pane only
keeps explorer, agents rail, transcript, editor, and composer render regions visually isolated
renders cursor, mode, command line, status, errors, fallback reason, provider identity, and Neovim cell highlights truthfully
handles narrow and short terminals without overlapping adjacent panes
does not rely on inline document state when the embedded Neovim provider is active
edgeCases: a long Neovim line is clipped to the pane width without extending into the agents rail
a command line longer than the pane width truncates inside the BUFFER footer
a Neovim line with highlight attributes renders cell foreground, background, and emphasis styles instead of dropping color
a one column pane renders without throwing and without negative widths
an inactive BUFFER pane shows provider status without stealing composer focus
long provider fallback, status, and error text truncates inside the BUFFER pane
a mouse scroll inside the editor pane leaves the project explorer scroll offset unchanged

requiredBehaviors: wraps the existing inline BUFFER store behind the provider API
labels inline mode as basic fallback in snapshots and status text
preserves existing open, edit, save, revert, hover, definition, undo, redo, and close behavior
keeps inline command handling scoped to fallback mode
reports fallback reasons from provider selection to the visible surface
edgeCases: inline fallback opens when Neovim discovery reports no usable executable
inline fallback save still blocks when an agent edit is in flight
inline fallback close refuses dirty edits unless discard is requested
inline fallback status text contains the fallback reason exactly once
inline fallback capabilities report terminal UI and exact Vim semantics as unavailable

requiredBehaviors: keeps external editor launch as an explicit action from the BUFFER provider surface
resolves editor commands from VISUAL, EDITOR, and known fallbacks without changing embedded provider defaults
blocks external editor handoff when the active provider has dirty embedded edits that have not been saved or discarded
refreshes the active provider snapshot after an external editor exits
surfaces external editor launch failures without terminating the TUI
edgeCases: a quoted editor command with arguments launches the executable and preserves arguments
a missing external editor executable returns a clear error status
external editor handoff is refused while embedded provider reports dirty state
external editor exit with file changes reloads the inline fallback snapshot
external editor cancellation leaves the embedded Neovim child process untouched

requiredBehaviors: adds a PTY scenario that launches workbench mode with embedded Neovim BUFFER enabled
drives insert, edit, write, undo, dirty quit refusal, force quit, search navigation, visual yank paste, named register basics, macro replay, resize, mouse isolation, and process cleanup
verifies file contents on disk after Neovim write operations
verifies killing the TUI leaves no Neovim child process alive
verifies missing Neovim uses inline fallback with a clear visible reason
keeps advanced Vim command behavior owned by embedded Neovim in the PTY flow instead of the inline Vim layer
edgeCases: embedded Neovim undo changes file contents after write and before later edits continue
dirty colon q keeps the workbench running and shows the Neovim refusal
colon q bang exits BUFFER without writing edited text
resizing during insert mode preserves typed text, cursor position, and Neovim grid visibility
mouse wheel over BUFFER does not move the workspace tree rows
mouse click over BUFFER moves the Neovim cursor without moving the workspace tree rows
a forced TUI kill while Neovim has dirty edits cleans up the embedded child process without writing dirty text

requiredBehaviors: runs TypeScript typecheck as a completion gate
runs production unused-code analysis as a completion gate
runs focused row tests, PTY validation, and visual smoke through row commands
runs the runtime build and package entrypoint verification as a completion gate
runs the local TUI runtime startup validation gate before completion
requires every row to declare executable commands and runs them during final checker execution
provides a project-local implementation-contract checker wrapper for final review enforcement
edgeCases: typecheck must pass after generated Neovim and workbench files are included
unused-production analysis must pass with the new provider modules and scripts reachable
build must regenerate runtime dist and verify package entrypoints
TUI runtime startup validation must run from this repository path after source changes
the project-local checker wrapper must enforce inventory, edge cases, reviews, row-command presence, and row-command execution by default

requiredBehaviors: documents embedded Neovim as the default BUFFER provider when a usable binary exists
documents configuration knobs for executable path, clean mode, user init mode, fallback mode, and timeouts
documents visible fallback reasons and troubleshooting steps
documents that inline mode is a basic fallback and external editor is an explicit handoff
documents the validation commands that protect the embedded Neovim BUFFER surface
edgeCases: documentation states missing executable behavior without suggesting exact Vim behavior in inline mode
documentation states unsupported version behavior with the user-facing fallback message
documentation states how to disable user init loading while preserving the default user config preference
documentation states process cleanup behavior after a TUI crash
documentation lists the contract checker and PTY validation commands
